info.name: F. FBM - Shipping & Inventory 🚚
info.description:
# FBM - Shipping & Inventory 🚚

## Description

End-to-end workflows for sellers who fulfill orders themselves. This collection covers multi-location inventory management, buying shipping labels through Amazon, and processing Easy Ship orders — everything you need to run FBM operations via SP-API.

## Overview

[Fulfillment by Merchant (FBM)](https://sell.amazon.com/programs/fulfilled-by-merchant), also known as Merchant Fulfilled Network (MFN), allows sellers to handle end-to-end fulfillment using their own logistics or third-party carriers. After listing products on Amazon, sellers manage shipping when a customer places an order.

To enhance efficiency, sellers can leverage Shipping Settings Automation (SSA) for accurate delivery estimates, Shipping Templates to configure rates, and Buy Shipping to access Amazon's negotiated carrier rates.

🎥 Learn more about FBM: [Watch the video](https://youtu.be/_lzmMQVysRQ?si=c5viZKc8AXxD6ROz)

## SP-APIs Involved

| API | Version | Documentation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Supply Sources API | v2020-07-01 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/supply-sources-api-v2020-07-01-reference) |
| Listings Items API | v2021-08-01 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/listings-items-api-v2021-08-01-reference) |
| Shipping API | v2 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/shipping-api-v2-reference) |
| Orders API | v0 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/orders-api-v0-reference) |
| External Fulfillment API | v2024-09-11 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/external-fulfillment-api-v2024-09-11-reference) |

## Use Cases

| Folder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| \[Inventory\] Multi-Location Inventory Flow | Set up supply sources, assign inventory per location, and provide location-level stock data to Amazon |
| \[Shipping\] Buy Shipping Flow | Purchase shipping labels through Amazon's partnered carriers at negotiated rates, confirm shipments, and track packages |
| \[External Fulfillment\] Easy Ship Order Processing | Process and fulfill Amazon Easy Ship eligible shipments using the External Fulfillment API |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- A registered SP-API application ([How to register](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/registering-your-application))
    
- Valid SP-API credentials (LWA client ID and secret)
    
- A seller account authorized to your application
    

### Setup Steps

1. **Fork the collection** — Click "Fork" on the collection to create your own copy.
    
2. **Fork the environment** — Fork the associated [environment template](https://www.postman.com/amazon-selling-partner-api/sp-api/environment/) as well.
    
3. **Set environment variables** — Fill in your `client_id`, `client_secret`, `refresh_token`, `marketplace`, and `base_url` in the environment.
    
4. **Assign the environment** — Select the forked environment from the environment dropdown in Postman.
    
5. **Set collection variables** — Configure the variables below for your use case.
    

### Collection Variables

| Variable | Description | Required For |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `mli.sku` | The SKU for inventory management | Multi-Location Inventory |
| `mli.seller_id` | Your seller ID | Multi-Location Inventory |
| `mli.marketplace_id` | Target marketplace ID | Multi-Location Inventory |
| `mli.locale` | Locale (e.g., `en_US`) | Multi-Location Inventory |
| `bs.order_id` | The order ID to ship | Buy Shipping |
| `bs.tracking_id` | Tracking ID returned after purchase | Buy Shipping |
| `bs.rate_id` | Selected shipping rate ID | Buy Shipping |
| `bs.carrier_id` | Carrier ID for the shipment | Buy Shipping |
| `es.shipmentId` | Easy Ship shipment ID | Easy Ship |
| `es.packageId` | Easy Ship package ID | Easy Ship |

## Additional Resources

- [SP-API Documentation](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/)
    
- [Buy Shipping](https://sell.amazon.com/tools/buy-shipping)
    
- [Marketplace IDs](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/marketplace-ids)

item.description:
## 🚚 What is Multi-Location Inventory (MLI)?

Multi-Location Inventory (MLI) is part of the FBM and allows sellers to provide **location-level inventory data** to Amazon. By sharing stock per location, sellers can:

- Show **accurate delivery estimates** to customers.
    
- **Optimize fulfillment** by selecting the nearest inventory location.
    
- Maintain **precise stock visibility** across multiple sites.
    
- Supply Sources = Inventory Locations
    

MLI integrates with **Shipping Settings Automation (SSA)** and requires shipping templates to be created and assigned in Seller Central.

> 🎥 Find more information on MLI [here](https://sell.amazon.com/programs/multi-location-inventory). 
  

---

## 🏬 How MLI Helps Sellers

MLI ensures sellers:

- **Improved delivery promises** → Customers see realistic timelines → Better **customer trust** through transparent inventory availability.
    
- **Optimized operations** → Orders can be routed to the most efficient supply source → Higher **on-time delivery rates** with accurate SSA-based shipping promises.
    
- **Accurate stock management** → Avoids overselling by syncing per-location inventory → **Reduced cancellations and returns** due to mismatched stock information.
    

---

## 🔑 Key APIs in the MLI Flow

To set up and manage MLI, sellers must integrate:

- [Supply Sources API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/supply-sources-api) → Create and manage supply sources (inventory locations).
    
- [Listings Items API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/listings-items-api) → Update inventory counts at each location.
    
- [Feeds API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/building-listings-management-workflows-guide#create-and-update-listings-items-in-bulk) → Perform bulk inventory and fulfillment updates.
    

---

## 🧰 MLI Postman Collection

This Postman collection demonstrates the **end-to-end MLI workflow**, showcasing how to integrate multiple supply sources and update location-level inventory for FBM SKUs.

> 🚨 If you want to test the Collection without the Postman Flow linked to it, make sure to go to the body requests and replace the variables that are marked between "{{}}" with the values that you need. 
  
> **🚨 Note that in Step 5** - you will need to enable Shipping Template and assign it manually to the SKU that you want to enable MLI For 
  

---

## 🌊 MLI Postman Flow

This collection also includes a **visualized MLI flow** in Postman Flows, guiding you through:

1. Setting up a new supply source.
    
2. Updating and verifying inventory across locations.
    

---

### 🧪 How to Test This Flow

✅ **Prerequisites**

1. **Set Environment Variables**
    
    - `client_id` → Your SP-API App Client ID
        
    - `client_secret` → Your SP-API App Client Secret
        
    - `refresh_token` → Valid SP-API Refresh Token
        
    - `marketplace` → Marketplace ID
        
2. **Set Collection Variables**
    
    - `seller_id`→ Your seller_id
        
    - `sku`→ Your SKU that you want to update
        

---

### 🏗️ **Running the Flow**

1. Go to the **Flows tab**.
    
2. Select the **\[MLI\] Multi-Location Inventory Flow**.
    
3. Enter required details in the input:
    
    1. `supplySourcePhone`
        
    2. `supplySourceEmail`
        
    3. `supplySourceAddress`
        
    4. `supplySourceAlias`
        
    5. `supplySourceQuantity`
        
    6. `supplySourceCode`
        
4. Save and Run the flow.
    

> 🚨 There are a lot of other variables that you can update for your supply source, you can do that by heading to Step 2. Update Supply Source in the collection, then Body and update the variables you want. You can update here things like opening times and timezone operating. 
  

You’ll get:

- A new Supply Source created with your settings.
    
- Assigning location level inventory to your SKU based on this new supply source
    
- Location-level inventory reflected in Amazon.
    
- As a final step, you will need validation of SSA shipping templates (via Seller Central).
    

---

## 💡 Notes & Tips

- Always **set DEFAULT channel inventory to 0** for SKUs managed under MLI.
    
- Shipping templates must be **SSA-enabled** and assigned to SKUs via **Seller Central**.
    
- Use **Feeds API** for bulk updates across multiple SKUs and supply sources.
    
- You can also Archive a supply source with the \[Optional\] request in the collection.

request.description:
Returns detailed order item information for the order that you specify. If NextToken is provided, it's used to retrieve the next page of order items.

__Note__: When an order is in the Pending state (the order has been placed but payment has not been authorized), the getOrderItems operation does not return information about pricing, taxes, shipping charges, gift status or promotions for the order items in the order. After an order leaves the Pending state (this occurs when payment has been authorized) and enters the Unshipped, Partially Shipped, or Shipped state, the getOrderItems operation returns information about pricing, taxes, shipping charges, gift status and promotions for the order items in the order.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 0.5 | 30 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values then those shown here. For more information, see [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](doc:usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

request.description:
Purchases a shipping service and returns purchase related details and documents.

Note: You must complete the purchase within **10 minutes** of rate creation by the shipping service provider. If you make the request after the 10 minutes have expired, you will receive an error response with the error code equal to "TOKEN_EXPIRED". If you receive this error response, you must get the rates for the shipment again.

**Usage Plan:**

| Default Quota | Unit |
| ---- | ---- |
| 5 | Requests Per Second |

The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values then those shown here. For more information, see [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

item.description: ## 🚚 What is Amazon Buy Shipping? [Buy Shipping](https://sell.amazon.com/tools/buy-shipping) is our built-in shipping label purchase platform where you can buy a shipping service from Amazon partnered carriers at the lowest available cost. Buy Shipping allows you to buy shipping labels individually or in bulk, ship and confirm your orders, and track your shipments. Using Buy Shipping helps safeguard your account from: 1. **Competitive rates:** Buy Shipping offers Amazon negotiated rates to sellers offering competitive pricing when compared to public rates. 2. **On time delivery:** Buy Shipping only suggests shipping services that can meet the delivery promise to the customer. 3. **Simplified booking process:** No need to visit the carrier’s website for logistics booking. Select orders to be shipped and book shipments from the same Seller Central workflow. 4. **Fully tracked solution:** Automatic shipping confirmation for orders without having to manually re-enter the tracking ID, while ensuring 100% compliance with Valid Tracking Rate. 5. **A-to-z guarantee claims:** If you purchase Amazon’s Buy Shipping and ship on time, you are protected against claims where a customer reports problems with delivery. Amazon will cover the cost of these claims and they will not affect your order defect rate. 6. **Negative customer feedback:** If you ship on time with tracking via Buy Shipping and the customer leaves seller feedback only on delayed or undelivered packages, you can request to have the feedback reviewed. To start using Buy Shipping, you have to open accounts with any of our integrated carrier partners and get the account linked to your Seller Central account. > 🎥 Find more information on Buy Shipping [here](https://youtu.be/gHN4V2K38ZA?si=wSb5hhGc187NbdbC). ## 🔑 Key APIs in the Merchant Fulfillment Flow A well-architected Amazon Merchant Fulfillment workflow relies on several key APIs - [Orders API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/orders-api-v0-reference) – Retrieves order details, including customer information, payment status, and fulfillment preferences, forming the foundation of your fulfillment process. - [Notifications API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/notifications-api-v1-reference) – Enables real-time event tracking, such as order confirmations, shipping updates, and inventory changes, ensuring timely and automated responses. - [Shipping API v2](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/amazon-shipping/docs/shipping-api-v2-reference) – Provides access to Amazon’s shipping services, allowing you to compare rates, purchase labels, and track shipments efficiently. ## 💡 Business Insights Buy Shipping service offers sellers a powerful solution to enhance their merchant-fulfilled operations with FBA-like efficiency. Sellers using this tool experience significant advantages: up to 31% reduction in shipping costs, 20% fewer potential late deliveries, and an average 31% better delivery promises. This comprehensive shipping solution helps sellers optimize their delivery performance while managing costs effectively, ultimately leading to improved buyer satisfaction and increased business growth. This Postman collection demonstrates the Buy Shipping workflow using the **Orders API and the Shipping API v2,** enabling sellers to purchase shipping for both **Prime** and **non-Prime** orders through Amazon’s Buy Shipping services. ### 🔨 Key Features - **Subscribe to Order Notifications**: Automate order tracking with `ORDER_CHANGE` notifications via the **Notifications API**. - **Retrieve Order Details**: Identify and fetch orders from Amazon using the **Orders API**. - **Get Shipping Offers**: Use `getRates` to preview available shipping services based on order details. - **Get Additional Inputs:** This step is only needed for rates that return that they need extra inputs. Mainly for international shipments. - **Purchase Shipments**: Select a shipping service and confirm the shipment using `purchaseShipment`, automatically deducting the cost from the seller’s account. - **Print Shipping Labels**: Extract and print shipping labels in supported formats (PDF, PNG, or ZPL). - **Cancel Shipments**: Use `cancelShipment` to void a shipment when necessary. - **Get Tracking Information**: Use the `getTracking` functionality to stay updated with the latest shipment status. ## 🌊 Buy Shipping Postman Flow This collection includes a fully visualized **Buy Shipping** flow using Postman Flows. It showcases each step as building blocks, guiding you from input to a successfully generated shipping label. ### 🛠️ What You’ll Need To test the flow, provide the following inputs: - `order_id`: A valid **Amazon Order Number** - **Package Details**: Weight and dimensions - **Label Dimensions** By default, the flow selects the **cheapest available shipping rate**, but you can customize this behavior directly in the flow logic. > 🎯 This is a great way to explore a **well-architected Buy Shipping flow**, understand how each API block fits together, and see the full journey from order to shipping label. ## 🚀 How to Test This Flow ### ✅ Prerequisites #### 1\. Set **Environment Variables** Go to the **Environment Variables** tab and enter the following: - `client_id`: Your SP-API App’s **Client ID** - `client_secret`: Your SP-API App’s **Client Secret** - `refresh_token`: A valid **SP-API Refresh Token** - `marketplace`: The **Marketplace ID** you're operating in #### 2\. Set **Collection Variables** In the **Collection Variables** tab, provide: - `order_id`: A valid **Amazon Order Number** - `shipping_preference`: Choose one from: `AmazonShipping_UK`, `AmazonShipping_US`, `AmazonShipping_UAE`, `AmazonShipping_EG`, `AmazonShipping_IN`, `AmazonShipping_JP`, `AmazonShipping_IT`, `AmazonShipping_FR`, `AmazonShipping_ES`, `AmazonShipping_SA` > ⚠️ Other optional variables (used in **(Optional)** requests like `cancelShipment` or `getTracking`) will be auto-generated when you run the flow. You can fill them in later if needed. --- ### 🧪 Running the Flow 1. Head to the **Flows** tab 2. Select the **\[Shipping\] Buy Shipping Flow** flow 3. Enter required details: - `order_id` - Package info (weight, dimensions) - Label dimensions 4. Hit **Run** You’ll get: - A generated **Shipment ID** - A **Tracking ID** - The **Shipping Label**, ready to go > 💡 Want to cancel the shipment? > Use the `cancelShipment` operation and make sure to copy the `shipmentId` from the flow results.

item.name: [External Fulfillment] Easy Ship Order Processing with External Fulfillment APIs
Step 1. Fetch open Easy Ship orders

query.description: (Required) Retrieves only those shipments which are in the specified status. The most common use-case would be to fetch all new shipments which would be in the ACCEPTED status. Valid values are ACCEPTED, CONFIRMED, PACKAGE_CREATED, PICKUP_SLOT_RETRIEVED, INVOICE_GENERATED, SHIPLABEL_GENERATED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED and CANCELLED.
The marketplace name associated with the location.
The Amazon location identifier for which shipments are to be retrieved
Shipments whose latest update is after the specified date/time are included in the response. This field should be in the ISO8601 date/time format.
Shipments whose latest update is before the specified date/time are included in the response. This field should be in the ISO8601 date/time format.
Specify the maximum number of shipments to be included in the response.
The nextToken value returned from a previous call to get shipments. Use this to retrieve the next page of shipments.

request.description: Get a list of shipments dropped for the seller in the specified status. Shipments can be further filtered based on the fulfillment node and/or shipments' last updated date and time.

query.description: (Required) The status in which shipment should be moved. Operations supported: CONFRIM or REJECT.

variable.description: (Required) The id of the shipment which is to be confirmed for fulfillment.

request.description: Confirms/Rejects that a seller will be fulfilling or cancelling the specified shipment.

variable.description: (Required) The id of the shipment for which package information is being provided.

request.description: Provides details about the packages that will be used to fulfill the specified shipment.

query.description: (Required) The id of the package for which available shipping option needs to be fetched. Note: The id of the package sent during CreatePackages (Step 3)

request.description: An API for a client to retrieve an optional list of shippingOptions that marketplace/channel provides for the pickup of the packages of an shipment. This API will return a list of shippingOptions if the marketplace/channel provides transportation and allows the seller to choose a shippingOption. If the marketplace/channel does not allow for a shippingOption to be selected, but has a pre-determined shippingOption, then this API will return an empty response.

query.description: The URL encoded id of the shippingOption for which the ship-labels are to be generated and retrieved. Only required for EasyShip channel. This is retrieved in Step 4.
(Required) Generation or regeneration of labels.

variable.description: (Required) The id of the shipment whose shipping labels are to be generated and retrieved.

request.description: Generates and retrieves all ship-labels for one or more packages in the specified shipment.

item.description: This collection compiles the steps required in order to process and fulfill Amazon Easy Ship eligible shipments using the External Fulfillment API suite on the Selling Partner API. --- ##### What is Amazon Easy Ship? Amazon Easy Ship is a fast, affordable and easy delivery service for Amazon sellers. When you choose Amazon Easy Ship, you need to drop-off Amazon orders at nearest eligible drop-off point and the carrier will deliver it to your customer’s doorstep. Your customers will get reliable and trackable deliveries. Amazon Easy Ship has a competitive rate card based on the package’s weight bands **What is External Fulfillment API?** The External Fulfilment API (also known as SmartConnect API) allows programmatic handling of inventory, returns, and shipping across multiple external fulfillment channels, including Seller Flex, Easy Ship, Self Ship, and MFN Self Delivery. For more information, visit the [documentation](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/external-fulfillment-shipping). --- **Authorization** The APIs in this collection require the _**x-amz-access-token**_ header in order for SP-API to authorize requests. The collection comes with a pre-script which calls the OAuth API to retrieve the access token and then stores it in the _access_token_ variable. The `Environments` tab in this workspace comes with environments for each API region and can be used with this collection. Make sure to select or fork the environment and fill in the values. **Variables** Variables required to run this collection are: - Authorization (environment level) - oauth_url - client_id - client_secret - refresh_token - access_token - grant_type - Marketplace (environment level) - marketplace - EasyShip logic (collection level) - es.shipmentStatus - es.shipmentId - _retrieved from shipments data (Step 1)_ - es.packageId - _fill this data with an id you select or generate_ - es.packageLineItemId - _retrieved from shipments data (Step1)_ - es.shippingOptionId - _retrieved from shipping options data (Step 4)_ **Important note:** If inventory was synced using the External Fulfillment Inventory API, make sure to set `shipmentStatus` to `ACCEPTED`. If inventory levels were pushed using other means such as the Listings Items API, the `shipmentStatus` must be set to `CREATED`.

variable.description: If inventory was synced using the External Fulfillment Inventory API, make sure to set shipmentStatus to ACCEPTED. If inventory levels were pushed using other means such as the Listings Items API, the shipmentStatus must be set to CREATED.
Id of the shipment.  Retrieved from shipments data (Step 1)
Fill this data with an id you select or generate
Retrieved from line items in shipments data (Step1)
The id of the shipping option selected in Step 4
